category_title: Students with ASD who exhibit school avoidance or distress during schedule changes
minimal_context: The student is preparing to transition to an unexpected school assembly in the high school setting.
dialogue:
TUTOR: There is a change in our class schedule today. We are going to a school assembly instead of our regular class.

STUDENT: But my agenda says it is time for regular class. I want to follow the routine and consistency.

TUTOR: The schedule is different today. We need to go to the assembly.

STUDENT: I can’t handle that change in schedule. I just don't want to go.

TUTOR: We have to go out into the hallway and head to the assembly now.

STUDENT: (Begins flapping hands and spinning) It is too chaotic and noisy out there! I am having a difficult time adjusting!

options.A: Implement cognitive self-regulation strategies where the student identifies the problem, states the reason for the problem, and suggests a solution.
options.B: Implement the creation of personalized stories where the student reads about unexpected schedule changes, school assemblies, and the noisy hallway environment.
options.C: Implement modeling with puppets where the student observes demonstrations of how to handle unexpected schedule changes and the noisy hallway environment.
options.D: Implement the use of large fonts with supporting pictures, reductions in the number of questions to answer, access to word banks, and speech-generating devices.
